title: Initial Snapshot
description: Database administrator triggers and monitors a full initial table snapshot via the Debezium Binlog Connector.

actors.name: DatabaseAdministrator
actors.description: Configures snapshot mode, triggers initial snapshot, monitors progress via notifications

actors.name: ConnectorFramework
actors.description: Debezium internal framework that orchestrates snapshot lifecycle (BaseSourceTask, ServiceRegistry)

actors.name: NotificationChannel
actors.description: Downstream channel (Kafka topic or log) that receives snapshot status notifications

use_cases.name: Configure Snapshot Mode
use_cases.description: DBA sets snapshot.mode in connector config (initial, schema_only, never, custom, etc.)
use_cases.details: Reads connector config; if mode=custom, uses snapshot.mode.custom.name; resolves Snapshotter via ServiceLoader

use_cases.name: Provision Snapshotter Service
use_cases.description: Framework instantiates SnapshotterService with resolved Snapshotter, SnapshotQuery, and SnapshotLock
use_cases.details: Looks up BeanRegistry → CommonConnectorConfig → loads Snapshotter via ServiceLoader → injects BeanRegistry if BeanRegistryAware → calls snapshotter.configure(map) → new SnapshotterService(snapshotter, snapshotQuery, snapshotLock)

use_cases.name: Initiate Full Table Snapshot
use_cases.description: Framework invokes snapshot source; BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource prepares BinlogSnapshotContext and fires STARTED notification

use_cases.name: Acquire Database Lock
use_cases.description: Connector acquires global read lock (or per-table locks) to ensure consistent snapshot
use_cases.details: Sets TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ; tries FLUSH TABLES WITH READ LOCK (globalLock) or FLUSH TABLES <list> WITH READ LOCK (tableLock); starts lock heartbeat every 30s; records globalLockAcquiredAt / tableLockAcquiredAt; calls metrics.setGlobalLockAcquired()

use_cases.name: Capture Table Schema
use_cases.description: Connector reads DDL for each captured table and emits schema change events
use_cases.details: SHOW CREATE TABLE per table; optional two-phase schema capture if twoPhaseSchemaSnapshot(); parallel DDL reading via ExecutorService if locking disabled; schema events dispatched via dispatcher.dispatchSchemaChangeEvent()

use_cases.name: Determine Binlog Snapshot Offset
use_cases.description: Connector records current binlog position so streaming can resume exactly after snapshot

use_cases.name: Release Schema Locks
use_cases.description: Minimal-locking mode: global read lock released early after schema is captured, before data rows are read
use_cases.details: Calls globalUnlock() if isGloballyLocked() and minimal locking enabled; table locks retained until data read completes

use_cases.name: Snapshot Table Data
use_cases.description: Connector reads all rows from each captured table and dispatches change events
use_cases.details: getSnapshotSelect() delegates to snapshotterService.getSnapshotQuery().snapshotQuery(tableId, columns); large tables use forward-only cursor with MIN_VALUE fetch size; row count estimated via connection.getEstimatedTableSize()

use_cases.name: Monitor Snapshot Progress
use_cases.description: DBA observes per-table and per-chunk progress notifications emitted during snapshot
notification_methods: notifyTableInProgress(partition, offsetContext, currentCollection, tables) → IN_PROGRESS + data_collections
notifyTableChunkInProgress(partition, offsetContext, currentCollection, chunkIndex, totalChunks, totalRowsScanned) → TABLE_CHUNK_IN_PROGRESS

use_cases.name: Release Data Locks
use_cases.description: After all table data is read, all remaining locks are released and delayed schema events processed
use_cases.details: globalUnlock() or tableUnlock() (UNLOCK TABLES); stops lock heartbeat; if delayedSchemaSnapshotTables non-empty, re-captures schema and dispatches events

use_cases.name: Complete Snapshot
use_cases.description: Snapshot finalized: last event marked, COMPLETED notification sent
use_cases.details: lastEventProcessor marks last record with SNAPSHOT_KEY=last; calls super.postSnapshot()

use_cases.name: Receive Snapshot Completion Notification
use_cases.description: DBA receives final snapshot status: COMPLETED, ABORTED, or SKIPPED via notification channel

use_cases.name: Abort Snapshot
use_cases.description: Snapshot interrupted; identity function applied to last event and ABORTED notification sent

key_relationships: InitialSnapshotNotificationService builds Notification with aggregateType=Initial Snapshot
important_notes: Snapshot mode 'custom' uses snapshot.mode.custom.name to load a user-defined Snapshotter via ServiceLoader
Two-phase schema snapshot: if global lock not acquired and table locks enabled, schema captured in two rounds
Lock heartbeat runs every 30s (SELECT 1) to keep connection alive during long snapshots
Large tables (> largeTableRowCount rows) use ResultSet.TYPE_FORWARD_ONLY + MIN_VALUE fetchSize to avoid OOM
snapshotterService.getSnapshotLock().tableLockingStatement() drives which SQL lock statement is executed
Notifications include connector_name, status, scanned_collection, data_collections, total_rows_scanned, chunk_index, total_chunks
